goal: Build a CLI-first research operating system that matches Dexter on core agent capability and beats it on evidence quality, continuity, and investor usefulness.
principles: Favor measurable quality improvements over feature count.
Default to natural-language interaction and keep explicit commands limited to session control.
Optimize for persistent investor workflows, not just one-off research answers.
Keep integration boundaries loose so Dexter-compatible ingestion can be added without coupling codebases.
Require every final conclusion to be traceable to sources, intermediate outputs, and validation steps.
Persist research artifacts so work compounds over time.
Fail honestly when evidence is weak, stale, or contradictory.

north_star_capabilities.description: Stable daily-use terminal workflows centered on a natural-language shell with minimal session commands.
A terminal interaction model where the user mostly speaks naturally and Templeton routes intent instead of depending on task-specific slash commands.
Every run is stored with source packets, scratchpad logs, agent outputs, and final memo artifacts.
Changes are scored against benchmark tasks and extraction-specific golden cases before they are trusted.
Filings parsing is based on real section boundaries instead of loose sentence scanning.
Final memos expose thesis, valuation, risks, catalysts, monitoring items, confidence, and invalidation triggers with evidence backing.
Ticker history, thesis drift, and what-changed comparisons are first-class workflows.
Watchlists, portfolio context, notes, and monitoring workflows help the user decide where new capital should go.
Templeton stores the user's own notes, open questions, disconfirming evidence, and conviction changes alongside generated research.

phases.name: Terminal Product Spec
phases.objective: Lock the user interaction model before adding more task surface area.
deliverables: session-state model for active ticker, model, memory, and history
minimal slash-command list focused on session control
intent-routing contract between shell and research engine
completion_criteria: Product decisions no longer require adding a new command for each research action.
The shell can distinguish meta commands from natural-language research requests.
Future CLI work is explicitly guided by a stable interaction spec.

phases.name: Investor Workspace Spec
phases.objective: Define watchlists, portfolio context, notes, and monitoring as first-class objects before implementation sprawl resumes.
completion_criteria: The product has durable objects for watchlists, portfolio views, notes, and monitoring items.
Future shell work can route requests into investor-workspace workflows without inventing ad hoc state.
The workspace model supports both one-off research and multi-position capital allocation.

phases.name: Durable CLI Baseline
phases.objective: Make the terminal shell and CLI repeatable and persistent enough to support later evaluation and history workflows.
completion_criteria: Every investigate run writes plan, source packet, outputs, and scratchpad artifacts.
Chat can answer from saved history when a fresh run is not required.
Users can locate the latest run for a ticker from the CLI output.
The shell can reuse an active ticker across consecutive turns.

phases.name: Benchmark Harness
phases.objective: Measure quality before increasing system complexity.
deliverables: benchmark dataset for end-to-end research prompts
golden extraction cases for filings parsing and risk extraction
scoring utilities for answer quality, evidence quality, and freshness handling
completion_criteria: A code change can be evaluated on a fixed benchmark suite.
Regression tests catch weaker business and risk extraction.
Templeton and Dexter can be compared on the same question set.

phases.name: Section-Aware Filing Parser
phases.objective: Replace heuristic extraction with more reliable section-based parsing.
deliverables: section boundary detector for Business, MD&A, and Risk Factors
normalized extraction pipeline built on detected sections
noise rejection for tables of contents, definitions, boilerplate, and legal text
completion_criteria: Business summaries consistently come from Business or equivalent sections.
Risk outputs are thesis-relevant and materially less boilerplate-heavy.
Golden filing extraction tests improve versus the current baseline.

phases.name: Verifier And Repair Loop
phases.objective: Prevent unsupported confidence from reaching the user.
deliverables: verifier agent for stale data, unsupported claims, contradictions, and missing evidence
confidence downgrade logic tied to verifier outcomes
completion_criteria: Contradictions across agents are surfaced or resolved before synthesis.
Low-evidence runs degrade gracefully instead of overclaiming.
Verifier outcomes are stored in the scratchpad and final artifacts.

phases.name: Decision-Grade Memos
phases.objective: Produce investor-useful outputs instead of generic summaries.
deliverables: claim-level evidence references in the memo
what changed since last run block
clear invalidation triggers and monitoring items
completion_criteria: Users can read one memo and understand the case for and against ownership.
The final decision is evidence-backed and explicitly constrained by uncertainty.
Memos are comparable across time for the same ticker.

phases.name: Research Memory, Watchlists, And Monitoring
phases.objective: Make research compound across time, watchlists, and held positions.
deliverables: earnings preview and post-event diff workflows
completion_criteria: A user can inspect how the thesis changed across runs.
The system can explain why a stock moved from buy to watch or pass.
The user can organize tickers by theme, industry, or capital-allocation priority.
Monitoring workflows surface meaningful changes instead of rerunning everything blindly.

phases.name: Capital Allocation Workspace
phases.objective: Help the user decide where new money should go, not just how to analyze a single stock.
deliverables: portfolio bucket targets and capacity tracking
candidate comparison views for new capital
decision journal with user notes and thesis checkpoints
completion_criteria: The user can ask which watchlist names deserve deeper work or new capital right now.
Templeton can explain portfolio and industry exposure gaps.
The system can distinguish ready-to-buy names from names that still need research.

integration_strategy.why: Templeton should remain the durability, quality, and investor-judgment layer while staying able to ingest Dexter-style run outputs later.
near_term_actions: Keep Templeton-native run artifacts structured and stable.
Design benchmark inputs and outputs to allow Templeton versus Dexter comparisons.
Avoid deep codebase fusion until differentiated value is proven.

current_priority_queue.item: Finalize the investor workspace spec for watchlists, portfolio context, notes, and monitoring.
Extend the terminal shell contract to handle investor-workspace intents naturally.
Implement the first working watchlist and portfolio prototype.
Build benchmark harness and golden-case dataset.
Upgrade memo schema with evidence references and change tracking.

parity: Templeton matches Dexter on dependable CLI research workflows and basic live-data coverage.
differentiation: Templeton exceeds Dexter on evidence traceability, filing extraction quality, memo usefulness, thesis continuity, and investor workflow durability.
proof: Benchmark results show consistent quality gains on the same question set.
Users can inspect why a conclusion was reached and what changed over time.
Users can organize ideas, portfolio context, and monitoring signals in one workspace rather than scattered notes and prompts.
